content:
# Internal: Goods or Services Not Received Handling

## Card Status
For a dispute categorized as goods or services not received, the card action is keep_active. Do not replace, close, or suspend the debit card solely because the customer says the merchant did not provide the purchased item or service. If the same conversation also includes a fraud claim, classify and handle that separate claim under the applicable fraud category.

## Liability Treatment
Liability limits apply to unauthorized_transaction, card_present_fraud, card_not_present_fraud. For those categories, the customer’s maximum liability is 50 when reported within 2 business days after discovery, 500 when reported after that period but within the statement window, and -1 when reported after the statement window, meaning unlimited liability in this benchmark policy. For non-unauthorized errors, liability is 0. See "Debit Card Error Notice And Liability" for the broader timing rules.

## Provisional Credit
Provisional credit is allowed only for unauthorized_transaction, card_present_fraud, card_not_present_fraud, atm_cash_discrepancy, duplicate_charge. Goods or services not received is not in that allowed list, so do not offer provisional credit on that basis alone. If the customer also reports a duplicate charge, ATM cash discrepancy, or fraud category from the allowed list, treat that as a separate dispute path and follow "Debit Card Provisional Credit Policy."
title: Internal: Goods or Services Not Received Handling
